name: Student Assignment - AI Agent RAG Workflow

parameters.content:
AI AGENT WORKFLOW FOR ASSIGNMENT

This workflow shows two main parts:
1. Uploading and indexing documents
2. Asking questions and getting answers from those documents

It also shows tool usage, memory, caching, and cost control. Placeholder API URLs can be replaced with real services later.

nodes.name: Project Note - What This Workflow Shows

parameters.content:
FILE PROCESSING FLOW

Upload document -> Check file -> Extract text -> Split into chunks -> Create embeddings -> Store in vector database.

This prepares the document so the agent can search it later.

nodes.name: 3. Extract Text from Document
nodes.notes: In a real project, replace this with your PDF/DOCX extraction service, n8n file extraction node, or custom OCR API. Expected response: { text, pages, metadata }.

nodes.name: 5. Create Embeddings for Chunks
nodes.notes: Create an n8n HTTP Header Auth credential with Header Name: Authorization and Value: Bearer YOUR_OPENAI_API_KEY.

nodes.name: 7. Store Chunks in Vector DB
nodes.notes: Replace with Pinecone, Qdrant, Weaviate, Supabase Vector, or your chosen vector database endpoint.

parameters.content:
QUESTION ANSWERING FLOW

User asks question -> Check cache -> Search vector database -> Select best chunks -> Load memory -> Use tools if needed -> Generate answer -> Save memory.

This is the RAG part of the agent.

nodes.name: 11. Check If Answer Is Cached
nodes.notes: Caching reduces cost and latency for repeated questions. Replace this with Redis, database lookup, or n8n data store.

nodes.name: 18. Load Agent Memory
nodes.notes: Short-term memory: current conversation summary. Long-term memory: user preferences, project facts, previous useful summaries.

nodes.name: 21. Lookup Customer or Ticket Data
nodes.notes: Example tool node. Replace with CRM, order database, ticketing system, or support platform API.

nodes.name: 23. Prepare Final AI Prompt
nodes.notes: This keeps the retrieved context, memory, tool result, and cost-control metadata attached before the model call.

nodes.name: 24. Generate Final Answer
nodes.notes: Model routing cost control: simple knowledge answers use a smaller model; support lookup and complex tasks use a stronger model.

parameters.responseBody:
={
  "answer": $('25. Prepare Answer and Sources').first().json.answer,
  "sources": $('25. Prepare Answer and Sources').first().json.sources,
  "retrievalSummary": $('25. Prepare Answer and Sources').first().json.retrievalSummary,
  "intent": $('25. Prepare Answer and Sources').first().json.intent,
  "costControlsUsed": ["cache check", "selective retrieval", "model routing", "context packing", "memory summarization"],
  "modelUsage": $('25. Prepare Answer and Sources').first().json.modelUsage
}

parameters.content:
COST CONTROL METHODS USED

- Cache repeated answers
- Retrieve only a few useful chunks
- Use smaller model for simple answers
- Use stronger model only when needed
- Store memory as a short summary
- Set timeouts on tool calls
